articles.title: Bets on US-Iran ceasefire show signs of insider knowledge, say experts
articles.summary: Prediction market activity on US-Iran ceasefire bets is displaying unusual patterns that experts believe indicate traders are acting on non-public information. This suggests either serious information leaks from diplomatic circles or sophisticated actors are manipulating these markets to profit from geopolitical events before they become public.

articles.title: Lawmakers Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Congress wants to stop prediction markets from offering bets on sports games, introducing legislation that would create a clear wall between political forecasting and gambling. The move protects prediction markets from getting lumped in with daily fantasy sports scandals, but it also limits their potential revenue streams just as platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket are scaling up.

articles.title: NBA Most Improved Player According to Kalshi Markets: Jalen Duran vs. Deni Avdija
articles.summary: Kalshi's NBA Most Improved Player markets are highlighting a tight race between Pistons center Jalen Duren and Wizards forward Deni Avdija as the season's breakout candidates. The betting action reveals how prediction markets can spot emerging talent before traditional media narratives catch up, giving sharp bettors an edge on player prop futures across sportsbooks.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.

articles.title: Opinion | We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Someone's arguing that prediction markets shouldn't become the dominant force in decision-making across society. This matters because as markets gain mainstream adoption, pushback from traditional institutions and policymakers could shape how much influence they're allowed to have in everything from corporate strategy to government policy.

articles.title: Polymarket opening 'Situation Room' pop-up bar in DC. See when.
articles.summary: Polymarket is launching a "Situation Room" pop-up bar in Washington DC, capitalizing on election fever with a prediction market-themed drinking experience. The move signals Polymarket's push to go mainstream and build brand recognition beyond crypto-native bettors, potentially drawing regulatory attention as they court DC's political class.
